CLI summariesFromVisibility also filters AdvAffectedProjects + draft/pending/active + ExecutionStatus = "Running" and only uses disk for archived/closed counts. subagent_reports.topic: Validate ExecutionStatus = "Running" Visibility guard for /adv-status stale active rows

sources.label: Temporal Search Attributes (official docs)
sources.summary: ExecutionStatus is a default Search Attribute of type Keyword. Valid values: Running, Completed, Failed, Canceled, Terminated, ContinuedAsNew, TimedOut. With advanced Visibility you may combine default + custom Search Attributes in one List Filter.

sources.label: Temporal List Filter (official docs)
sources.summary: List Filter is an SQL-like string supporting =, IN, AND, OR, (). Documented examples combine WorkflowId/WorkflowType with ExecutionStatus = 'Running' / != 'Running' via AND. ExecutionStatus and string literals with double quotes are valid.

sources.label: Temporal Visibility (official docs)
sources.summary: Standard Visibility (limited default-only filters) deprecated v1.21, removed v1.24. All current deployments use advanced Visibility, which supports combining default and custom Search Attributes in one List Filter.

sources.label: Repo: bin/lib/live-status.ts summariesFromVisibility
sources.summary: Current CLI query is `AdvAffectedProjects = "<pid>" AND AdvChangeStatus IN ("draft","pending","active")` — relies solely on custom AdvChangeStatus, no execution-state guard.

sources.label: Repo: plugin/src/temporal/workflows.ts archive/close handlers
sources.summary: Archive upserts AdvChangeStatus=['archived'] then workflow Completes; but upsert is skipped when searchAttributesEnabled===false and any upsert error is caught+swallowed (logged warn). On those paths AdvChangeStatus stays 'active' while ExecutionStatus becomes Completed — exactly the leak.

sources.label: Repo grep: ExecutionStatus usage
sources.summary: ExecutionStatus is NOT used in any existing ADV Visibility query; this guard is net-new and does not conflict with existing query construction.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Existing pattern: CLI active rows filter only on the custom AdvChangeStatus Keyword. Reference pattern: Temporal docs explicitly support and demonstrate guarding active/inactive listings with the default ExecutionStatus = "Running" attribute, combined with custom attributes via AND under advanced Visibility (universal since Server v1.24). Deviation: MINOR and corrective. The proposed `AdvAffectedProjects = "<pid>" AND AdvChangeStatus IN ("draft","pending","active") AND ExecutionStatus = "Running"` is a boring, server-native, by-the-book filter. It makes correctness structural (server-side execution state) instead of trusting the swallowed custom-attribute upsert. Worker-free read path is preserved (still pure client.workflow.list, no getState query, no disk fallback). Root-cause note: custom AdvChangeStatus on archive is best-effort (try/catch + searchAttributesEnabled gate + poisoned/recovery disk-only archive paths that never fire the workflow signal), so any of those leaves a Completed workflow with stale active AdvChangeStatus. ExecutionStatus is set by the server on completion and cannot be swallowed, so it closes the leak class at the source.

validation.notes: ExecutionStatus = "Running" is a valid, documented default Search Attribute and List Filter term, combinable with custom attributes via AND under advanced Visibility (the only mode in any supported Temporal Server). Verdict: CONFIRMED. Edge cases to handle in implementation/tests: (1) ExecutionStatus is the ALIAS of the default attribute; aliasing of default SAs requires Server v1.30+ for arbitrary defaults, but ExecutionStatus has been usable unprefixed in List Filters in long-standing docs/CLI examples — if a target Temporal Server rejects the unprefixed name, fall back to TemporalExecutionStatus. Add a comment documenting both names. (2) Verify ADV has NOT registered a CUSTOM attribute named ExecutionStatus that would shadow the default (grep shows it has not). (3) ContinuedAsNew rotation: change workflows continue-as-new on history threshold (workflows.ts:1497) — a rotated workflow's prior run is Completed/ContinuedAsNew while the new run is Running, so listing by ExecutionStatus=Running still surfaces the live run; confirm the Visibility list returns the open run for CAN'd changes and does not drop a legitimately-active change mid-rotation. (4) Keyword exact-match: ExecutionStatus is Keyword, so `=` is exact; no tokenization risk. (5) String literal quoting: docs accept both single and double quotes; keep double quotes for consistency with existing query and the projectId escaping already present.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Proceed with the fix as designed. Append ` AND ExecutionStatus = "Running"` to the summariesFromVisibility query in bin/lib/live-status.ts. Keep it worker-free and disk-fallback-free. Add a code comment citing https://docs.temporal.io/list-filter and noting TemporalExecutionStatus as the Temporal-prefixed fallback name if an older/newer server rejects the unprefixed alias. Suggested test assertions: (a) the constructed query string contains `AdvChangeStatus IN ("draft", "pending", "active")` AND `ExecutionStatus = "Running"` joined by AND; (b) given a fake VisibilityListClient whose list() only yields executions matching the full query, a completed/archived workflow with stale AdvChangeStatus=["active"] is NOT yielded (i.e. the boundary excludes it at the query level, not in post-filter) — assert the test client's recorded query and that summaries excludes the stale id; (c) a genuinely-active draft/pending/active Running workflow IS included; (d) projectId double-quote escaping is preserved when ExecutionStatus is appended; (e) regression: query is a single AND-joined string with exactly one ExecutionStatus term. Optionally add a contract test in plugin/src/temporal to assert ADV registers no custom 'ExecutionStatus' attribute (prevents future shadowing).
follow_ups: Consider applying the same ExecutionStatus = "Running" guard to plugin/src/temporal/list-change-workflows.ts buildVisibilityQuery so MCP cold-start enumeration and CLI stay structurally aligned (out of this change's stated scope but same leak class).

Implementation should decide unprefixed `ExecutionStatus` vs `TemporalExecutionStatus`; if the project pins a Temporal Server version, verify which name that server accepts before locking the test assertion string.
Packet anchors TASK_SCOPE/IN_SCOPE/OUT_OF_SCOPE/DONE_WHEN/STOP_WHEN/VERIFICATION were not provided as explicit labeled blocks; proceeded with the inline TASK description as scope.

blocking_findings.label: issue
blocking_findings.what: `adv_status view:"changes"` active-list path is not structurally covered by the new `ExecutionStatus = "Running"` guard.
blocking_findings.why: Agreement requires `/adv-status` and `adv_status view:"changes"` to return the same active changes/count. Scoped diff only updates `bin/lib/live-status.ts`; MCP status still reaches `listChangeWorkflowIds`, whose query filters only `AdvAffectedProjects` and `AdvChangeStatus` and lacks the execution-status filter that excludes completed workflows with stale active search attributes.
blocking_findings.fix: Update the MCP/status visibility path (likely shared `listChangeWorkflowIds` or its status caller) to filter running executions where appropriate, and add a regression proving stale completed active workflows are excluded from `adv_status view:"changes"`.

nonblocking_findings.what: CLI regression directly records the visibility query and simulates stale completed workflow exclusion while retaining a running active workflow.
nonblocking_findings.why: This covers the reported stale custom search-attribute failure mode without requiring a live worker or disk fallback.

documents.proposal:
# Fix stale status

## Intent
Fix `/adv-status` CLI active table so archived or completed changes cannot appear as active when Temporal Visibility custom search attributes are stale.

## LBP Targets
- Preserve worker-free Temporal Visibility read path for default CLI status.
- Filter active rows structurally using Temporal Visibility execution state, not disk fallback.
- Keep MCP `adv_status view:"changes"` and CLI `/adv-status` active rows aligned.

## Scope
- `bin/lib/live-status.ts`
- `bin/lib/live-status.test.ts`
- Contract tests only if needed to lock behavior.

## User Outcomes
- `/adv-status` active count and rows match `adv_status view:"changes"` for active changes in the same project/session.
- Archived changes are excluded from `/adv-status` active table.
- Regression coverage prevents archived/completed workflows with stale active custom attributes from leaking into active output.

## Spec-law impact
No spec law update required: existing `advance-meta` requirements already require `/adv-status` to be live Temporal-backed, fail closed instead of stale fallback, and exclude terminal-complete changes from active rows. This fix enforces that existing law more structurally.
documents.problemStatement:
# Problem statement

`/adv-status` can show archived ADV changes as active. In pokeedge-web, `/adv-status` reported 18 active changes while fresh MCP reads showed 7 active; several rows shown by `/adv-status` were already archived via `adv_change_list`.

Expected: `/adv-status` active table matches authoritative active state and never lists archived changes.

Actual: CLI active rows can diverge from MCP state, likely because the CLI reads custom Temporal Visibility search attributes without also requiring the workflow execution to still be running.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement

## Objectives
- Exclude archived/completed change workflows from `/adv-status` active rows.
- Preserve worker-free Temporal Visibility status path.
- Keep disk projections out of active-row authority.
- Add regression coverage for stale custom search attributes on completed workflows.

## Acceptance criteria
- Running `/adv-status` and `adv_status view:"changes"` for the same project within the same session returns the same active changes and active count.
- Archived changes are excluded from the active table.
- Regression test covers a completed/archived workflow with stale active `AdvChangeStatus` / non-done gate attributes and proves it is excluded.
- If status output uses a cache, freshness/cached-at is surfaced or invalidated after archive; if no cache backs CLI active rows, test/rationale states so.

## Constraints
- No new dependency.
- No disk fallback as source for active rows.
- Default `/adv-status` remains thin CLI bridge over `adv status --no-color`.

## Out of scope
- Reworking MCP `adv_status` response shape.
- Changing archive workflow semantics beyond status visibility filtering.
documents.design:
# Design

## Approach
Update the CLI Visibility query used by `summariesFromVisibility` to require both:

- `AdvChangeStatus IN ("draft", "pending", "active")`
- `ExecutionStatus = "Running"`

Rationale: archived/closed change workflows complete after terminal status. Temporal exposes `ExecutionStatus` as a default Search Attribute and supports list filters such as `ExecutionStatus = "Running"`. Adding this guard keeps the CLI worker-free and live-Temporal-backed while excluding completed workflows even if custom `AdvChangeStatus` is stale.

## Validation evidence
- Internal spec `advance-meta` `rq-statusCliBridge01` and `rq-statusCliWorkerFree01` require live CLI status, no stale disk active fallback, worker-free Visibility active rows, and terminal-complete exclusion.
- Temporal docs confirm `ExecutionStatus` is a default Search Attribute with values including `Running` and `Completed`, and TypeScript/Visibility APIs accept list filter queries such as `ExecutionStatus = "Running"`.
- Independent `adv-researcher` verdict: CONFIRMED. It identified the same root cause: archive upsert of custom `AdvChangeStatus` is best-effort, while server-set `ExecutionStatus` cannot be swallowed.

## Implementation notes
- Add `ExecutionStatus = "Running"` to `summariesFromVisibility` query construction.
- Add regression test asserting the query contains the execution-state guard.
- Add regression test with fake Visibility client honoring `ExecutionStatus`: completed workflow with stale active custom attributes is excluded; running active workflow is included.
- Keep active counts based on returned summaries; terminal counts remain from archive disk bundles as existing CLI behavior.

## Spec-law impact
No spec law update required: this enforces existing `advance-meta` status CLI law. No new behavior or contract shape is introduced.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary

Fixed stale `/adv-status` active-change output by requiring Temporal server-owned running execution state on active Visibility queries.

## What changed
- CLI `/adv-status` active rows now query `ExecutionStatus = "Running"` alongside active ADV status search attributes.
- Shared `listChangeWorkflowIds` active enumeration now uses the same running-execution guard, keeping MCP/status parity while preserving `statuses: null` terminal/audit mode.
- Added regression coverage for completed workflows with stale active custom search attributes.

## Verification
- RED: targeted status tests failed before the guard.
- GREEN: `bun test bin/lib/live-status.test.ts` passed 13 tests.
- VERIFY: `bun test bin/` passed 107 tests.
- VERIFY: `bin/oc-test targeted -- src/temporal/list-change-workflows.test.ts src/storage/store-temporal/index.test.ts` passed 33 tests.
- Independent review verdict: READY.

| AC3 | acceptance_criterion | Regression test covers a completed/archived workflow with stale active `AdvChangeStatus` / non-done gate attributes and proves it is excluded. | pass | RED/GREEN tests added for stale completed workflow exclusion and shared active visibility query guard; targeted and bin suites pass. |
| AC4 | acceptance_criterion | If status output uses a cache, freshness/cached-at is surfaced or invalidated after archive; if no cache backs CLI active rows, test/rationale states so. | pass | No cache added to CLI active rows; `loadLiveSummaries` still creates Temporal client and executes `summariesFromVisibility` per call. Active rows remain live Visibility-backed. |

| OOS1 | out_of_scope | Reworking MCP `adv_status` response shape. | respected | No changes to `plugin/src/tools/status.ts` response shape. |
